feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation on SCL/SDA.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use supported direct mode only; verify observable bus timing on SCL and SDA.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps operation.
Enable the core and initiate a representative I2C transfer.
Measure the SCL clock rate during the transfer on the external bus.
Confirm SDA data transfer occurs with the generated 100 Kbps SCL timing.
expected_results: The core performs an I2C transfer successfully.
Measured SCL rate corresponds to 100 Kbps normal-mode operation.
SDA activity is synchronized with the 100 Kbps SCL clock.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation on SCL/SDA.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use supported direct mode only; verify observable bus timing on SCL and SDA.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps operation.
Enable the core and initiate a representative I2C transfer.
Measure the SCL clock rate during the transfer on the external bus.
Confirm SDA data transfer occurs with the generated 400 Kbps SCL timing.
expected_results: The core performs an I2C transfer successfully.
Measured SCL rate corresponds to 400 Kbps fast-mode operation.
SDA activity is synchronized with the 400 Kbps SCL clock.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_description: Verify presence and basic operation of the specified WISHBONE Classic interface signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Verification limited to signals and behavior explicitly provided in the specification.
test_steps: Drive WISHBONE transactions using wb_clk_i, wb_rst_i, wb_adr_i, wb_dat_i, wb_we_i, wb_stb_i, and wb_cyc_i.
Perform at least one write transaction and one read transaction.
Observe wb_ack_o response for each access.
Observe wb_dat_o during read transactions and wb_inta_o during interrupt-related scenarios.
expected_results: The specified WISHBONE interface signals are used by the core.
Read and write accesses complete through the WISHBONE interface.
wb_ack_o acknowledges accesses and wb_dat_o returns read data.
wb_inta_o is available for interrupt signaling.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_description: Verify that each WISHBONE access completes in two wb_clk_i cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Measure cycle count from valid access assertion to acknowledge using wb_clk_i.
test_steps: Initiate a WISHBONE write access by asserting wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i with valid address and data.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
Repeat for a WISHBONE read access.
Compare observed cycle counts for both accesses.
expected_results: Each write access takes two wb_clk_i cycles.
Each read access takes two wb_clk_i cycles.
wb_ack_o is asserted on the second clock cycle of each access.

feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level must be configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify configurable asynchronous reset polarity through ARST_LVL and arst_i.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires separate verification runs for ARST_LVL = 1'b1 and ARST_LVL = 1'b0.
test_steps: Instantiate/configure the core with ARST_LVL set to active high.
Assert arst_i high and observe reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and confirm normal operation can resume.
Instantiate/configure the core with ARST_LVL set to active low.
Assert arst_i low and observe reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and confirm normal operation can resume.
expected_results: With ARST_LVL set active high, arst_i high causes reset.
With ARST_LVL set active low, arst_i low causes reset.
In both configurations, deasserting reset allows normal operation.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify presence and functional use of scl_pad_i/o/oe and sda_pad_i/o/oe.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only verify the external connection interface signals specified.
test_steps: Inspect the core interface for scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, scl_pad_oe, sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
Run a transfer and observe activity on the pad output and output-enable signals.
Drive input stimulus on scl_pad_i and sda_pad_i as needed and observe core response.
expected_results: All six pad interface signals are present.
The pad output and output-enable signals toggle consistently with bus activity.
The input signals are accepted by the core for bus state observation.

feature_list.requirement_text: Devices connected to SCL and SDA must use open-drain or open-collector outputs.
feature_list.test_description: Verify that the interface is intended for open-drain/open-collector style external connection.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is an external electrical system requirement; core-only verification is limited.
test_steps: Review the external connection behavior using SCL/SDA-related pad signals.
Confirm the core uses output-enable control rather than a push-pull drive model for bus connection.
Assess whether the interface supports release of the bus lines for shared-bus use.
expected_results: The core interface is compatible with open-drain/open-collector style connection.
The bus connection model allows line release for shared-bus operation.

feature_list.requirement_text: The SCL and SDA output-enable signals must control whether the core drives or releases the external bus lines.
feature_list.test_description: Verify that scl_pad_oe and sda_pad_oe control bus drive versus release behavior.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe relationship among pad_oe, pad_o, and external bus line behavior.
test_steps: Initiate bus activity that requires the core to drive SCL and SDA low.
Observe scl_pad_oe and sda_pad_oe during driven intervals.
Observe scl_pad_oe and sda_pad_oe during intervals when the core must release the bus.
Correlate output-enable state with whether the core is actively controlling or releasing the bus lines.
expected_results: When the core drives a bus line, the corresponding output-enable indicates active drive control.
When the core releases a bus line, the corresponding output-enable indicates release state.
Bus behavior matches output-enable control on both SCL and SDA.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERlo must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale low-byte register at address 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo address, width, and read/write access behavior.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use WISHBONE accesses at address 0x00 only.
test_steps: Write a test value to address 0x00 via WISHBONE.
Read back from address 0x00.
Repeat with a second 8-bit value to confirm full byte storage.
expected_results: Address 0x00 behaves as the PRERlo register.
The register accepts 8-bit write data.
Readback returns the last written 8-bit value.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERhi must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale high-byte register at address 0x01.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERhi address, width, and read/write access behavior.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use WISHBONE accesses at address 0x01 only.
test_steps: Write a test value to address 0x01 via WISHBONE.
Read back from address 0x01.
Repeat with a second 8-bit value to confirm full byte storage.
expected_results: Address 0x01 behaves as the PRERhi register.
The register accepts 8-bit write data.
Readback returns the last written 8-bit value.

feature_list.requirement_text: CTR must be an 8-bit read/write Control register at address 0x02.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR address, width, and read/write access behavior.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use WISHBONE accesses at address 0x02 only.
test_steps: Write a test value to address 0x02 via WISHBONE.
Read back from address 0x02.
Repeat with a second 8-bit value to confirm writable behavior.
expected_results: Address 0x02 behaves as the CTR register.
The register accepts 8-bit writes.
Readback returns the stored control value subject to any specified bit behavior.

feature_list.requirement_text: TXR must be an 8-bit write-only Transmit register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR exists at address 0x03 and supports write-only access.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read behavior is not specified for TXR because address 0x03 is also used by RXR for reads.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit test value to address 0x03 with wb_we_i asserted.
Repeat with a second 8-bit test value.
Observe that the write access completes successfully.
expected_results: Address 0x03 accepts write data as TXR.
TXR behaves as an 8-bit write-only transmit register.

feature_list.requirement_text: RXR must be an 8-bit read-only Receive register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR exists at address 0x03 and supports read-only access.
feature_list.test_constraints: RXR validity depends on prior receive activity or loaded receive data condition.
test_steps: Cause the core to receive a byte via I2C.
Perform a WISHBONE read from address 0x03.
Repeat after a different received byte if available.
expected_results: Address 0x03 returns RXR data on read accesses.
RXR behaves as an 8-bit read-only receive register.
Read data reflects the received byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: CR must be an 8-bit write-only Command register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR exists at address 0x04 and supports write-only access.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read behavior is not specified for CR because address 0x04 is also used by SR for reads.
test_steps: Write command patterns to address 0x04 with wb_we_i asserted.
Observe that command writes are accepted and affect core behavior.
Repeat with multiple supported command bit combinations.
expected_results: Address 0x04 accepts writes as the CR register.
CR behaves as an 8-bit write-only command register.

feature_list.requirement_text: SR must be an 8-bit read-only Status register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR exists at address 0x04 and supports read-only access.
feature_list.test_constraints: Status contents depend on core state during test.
test_steps: Read from address 0x04 via WISHBONE in idle state.
Exercise transfer activity and read address 0x04 again.
Confirm the returned value tracks status conditions rather than command writeback.
expected_results: Address 0x04 returns SR data on read accesses.
SR behaves as an 8-bit read-only status register.
Read data reflects current status conditions.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register must configure the SCL clock using the WISHBONE input clock and the 5SCL prescale formula.
feature_list.test_description: Verify that PRERlo/PRERhi program SCL frequency relative to wb_clk_i per the 5*SCL minus 1 rule.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires measurable wb_clk_i and SCL frequencies; exact formula application must be inferred from programmed values and observed output.
test_steps: Apply a known wb_clk_i frequency.
Program PRERlo/PRERhi with a selected prescale value.
Enable the core and initiate an I2C transfer.
Measure the resulting SCL frequency.
Repeat with a second prescale value and compare measured SCL to expected scaling.
expected_results: Changing the prescale register changes the generated SCL frequency.
Observed SCL frequency is derived from wb_clk_i according to the programmed prescale behavior.
Results are consistent with the specified internal 5*SCL prescale relationship.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register value must only be changed when the EN bit is cleared.
feature_list.test_description: Verify prescale register programming rule relative to EN bit state.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a software usage rule; hardware enforcement is not explicitly specified.
test_steps: Clear EN and write new values to PRERlo and PRERhi.
Set EN and attempt to write new values to PRERlo and PRERhi during enabled operation.
Observe whether prescale changes are accepted or whether usage must be constrained by software procedure.
expected_results: Prescale registers can be changed with EN cleared.
Behavior when EN is set is documented by test observation, but strict hardware blocking is not guaranteed by the requirement text.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register reset value must be 0xFFFF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRER reset value after reset.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check immediately after reset before software modification.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read PRERlo from address 0x00.
Read PRERhi from address 0x01.
Combine the bytes into the 16-bit prescale value.
expected_results: PRERlo resets to 0xFF.
PRERhi resets to 0xFF.
Combined Prescale Register reset value is 0xFFFF.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 7 must be the read/write EN bit used to enable or disable the I2C core.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR[7] is the RW EN control bit and that it enables/disables the core.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use register address 0x02 for CTR access.
test_steps: Write CTR with bit 7 cleared and read back CTR.
Attempt a new command with EN cleared.
Write CTR with bit 7 set and read back CTR.
Attempt a new command with EN set.
expected_results: CTR bit 7 is writable and readable as EN.
When EN is 0, the core is disabled for new command response.
When EN is 1, the core is enabled for new command response.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must only respond to new commands when EN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify that new commands are only acted upon when EN=1.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use CR writes as new command stimulus.
test_steps: Clear EN in CTR.
Write a command to CR that would normally initiate bus activity.
Observe whether bus activity or command execution occurs.
Set EN in CTR.
Write the same command to CR again and observe behavior.
expected_results: With EN cleared, the core does not respond to the new command.
With EN set, the core responds to the new command and corresponding activity occurs.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software must only clear EN when no transfer is in progress, after a STOP command, or when STO is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify observable behavior around clearing EN relative to transfer-in-progress conditions.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a software usage rule; hardware prohibition is not explicitly specified.
test_steps: Start a transfer and observe TIP asserted.
Attempt to clear EN while transfer is in progress and before STOP/STO condition.
Issue STOP or set STO and wait for transfer completion.
Clear EN after STOP/STO condition.
expected_results: The safe software sequence for clearing EN is after no transfer is in progress and after STOP/STO condition.
Any behavior when clearing EN mid-transfer is captured as observation, not strict pass/fail hardware enforcement.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 6 must be the read/write IEN bit used to enable or disable interrupt generation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR[6] is the RW IEN bit controlling interrupt generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Interrupt-generating condition must be created during the test.
test_steps: Write CTR with IEN cleared and read back CTR.
Create an interrupt-pending condition.
Observe wb_inta_o.
Write CTR with IEN set and read back CTR.
Create an interrupt-pending condition again and observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: CTR bit 6 is writable and readable as IEN.
With IEN=0, interrupt request is disabled.
With IEN=1, interrupt request is enabled when an interrupt-pending condition exists.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Control Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR reset value.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check immediately after reset before software modification.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read CTR from address 0x02.
expected_results: CTR resets to 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register must hold the byte to transmit, with bit 0 acting as the data LSB during data transfer or the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR content is used for transmitted byte formation, including bit 0 role.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observing transmitted SDA bits during both data and address transfers.
test_steps: Load TXR with a known data byte and initiate a data write transfer.
Observe the transmitted SDA bit sequence and confirm bit 0 is sent as the data LSB.
Load TXR with a known slave address byte including bit 0 set for RW encoding and initiate address transfer.
Observe the transmitted address byte and confirm bit 0 is used as the RW bit.
expected_results: TXR holds and transmits the programmed byte.
During data transfer, TXR bit 0 is the transmitted data LSB.
During slave address transfer, TXR bit 0 is transmitted as the RW bit.

feature_list.requirement_text: During a slave address transfer, RW value 1 must indicate reading from the slave and RW value 0 must indicate writing to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RW encoding in TXR[0] during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires address transfer observation and follow-on direction behavior.
test_steps: Load a slave address byte with TXR[0]=0 and initiate slave address transfer.
Observe that the transfer corresponds to write direction.
Load a slave address byte with TXR[0]=1 and initiate slave address transfer.
Observe that the transfer corresponds to read direction.
expected_results: RW=0 indicates writing to the slave.
RW=1 indicates reading from the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR reset value.
feature_list.test_constraints: TXR is write-only; direct readback is not specified.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Perform the first transmit operation without modifying TXR, or use an implementation-observable method tied to TXR content.
Assess whether the transmitted/default TXR content corresponds to 0x00.
expected_results: TXR reset behavior corresponds to value 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain the last byte received via I2C.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR stores the last received byte.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires receiving at least two distinct bytes for overwrite confirmation.
test_steps: Cause the core to receive a first byte via I2C.
Read RXR from address 0x03 and record the value.
Cause the core to receive a second, different byte via I2C.
Read RXR again.
expected_results: RXR contains the received byte after each receive operation.
After the second reception, RXR contains the last byte received.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR reset value.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check immediately after reset before any receive activity.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read RXR from address 0x03.
expected_results: RXR resets to 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 7 must be the write-only STA bit used to generate a START or Repeated START condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR[7] acts as write-only STA command bit for START generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Repeated START case requires an already active bus transaction without prior STOP.
test_steps: Write CR with STA and WR or RD set.
Observe bus activity on SDA and SCL for START condition generation.
During an active transaction without STOP, write CR with STA and WR or RD set again.
Observe generation of a repeated START condition.
expected_results: CR bit 7 functions as the STA command bit.
Writing STA with a transfer command generates a START.
Writing STA again during an active transaction generates a Repeated START.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 6 must be the write-only STO bit used to generate a STOP condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR[6] acts as write-only STO command bit for STOP generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires an active or pending transfer context where STOP is observable.
test_steps: Start an I2C transfer.
Write CR with STO set.
Observe SDA and SCL for STOP condition generation.
expected_results: CR bit 6 functions as the STO command bit.
Writing STO generates a STOP condition on the bus.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 5 must be the write-only RD bit used to initiate a read from the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR[5] acts as write-only RD command bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires slave read scenario or modeled read response.
test_steps: Set up the core for a read transaction.
Write CR with RD set.
Observe that the core initiates a read from the slave and captures received data.
expected_results: CR bit 5 functions as the RD command bit.
Writing RD initiates a read operation from the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 4 must be the write-only WR bit used to initiate a write to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR[4] acts as write-only WR command bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires transmit data available in TXR.
test_steps: Load TXR with a test byte.
Write CR with WR set.
Observe that the core initiates a write to the slave and transmits the TXR byte.
expected_results: CR bit 4 functions as the WR command bit.
Writing WR initiates a write operation to the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 3 must be the write-only ACK bit used by a receiver to send ACK when 0 or NACK when 1.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR[3] controls ACK/NACK value sent by receiver.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires receiver-side read completion scenario.
test_steps: Initiate a read operation where the core acts as receiver.
Write CR with ACK bit = 0 for the response phase.
Observe the acknowledge bit driven on SDA during the ninth clock.
Repeat with ACK bit = 1.
Observe the no-acknowledge bit driven on SDA during the ninth clock.
expected_results: ACK=0 causes an ACK response.
ACK=1 causes a NACK response.
CR bit 3 functions as the receiver acknowledge control bit.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 0 must be the write-only IACK bit used to clear a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR[0] clears a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires an interrupt-pending condition before issuing IACK.
test_steps: Create a condition that sets IF.
If IEN is enabled, observe wb_inta_o asserted.
Write CR with IACK set.
Read SR and observe IF status; observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: Writing IACK clears the pending interrupt flag.
If asserted, wb_inta_o deasserts after interrupt acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify automatic clearing and read-as-zero behavior of transient command bits.
feature_list.test_constraints: Readback must be inferred via command/status interface behavior because CR shares address 0x04 with SR on reads.
test_steps: Write CR with STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK bits asserted in applicable scenarios.
Allow command processing to proceed.
Perform a read from address 0x04 and verify that command bits are not returned as set through the read path.
Repeat for each command bit individually if needed.
expected_results: STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK clear automatically after command acceptance.
These command bits are not readable as ones through the read path and effectively read as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Command Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR reset value.
feature_list.test_constraints: CR is write-only; direct readback is not specified.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Use implementation-observable behavior to confirm that no command bits are active after reset.
expected_results: CR reset behavior corresponds to 0x00 with no active command state.

feature_list.requirement_text: RxACK must indicate whether acknowledge was received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RxACK status semantics for acknowledge and no-acknowledge.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires both ACK and NACK slave response cases.
test_steps: Perform a transfer to an addressed slave that responds with ACK.
Read SR and inspect RxACK bit.
Perform a transfer where no acknowledge is returned.
Read SR and inspect RxACK bit again.
expected_results: RxACK=0 when acknowledge is received from the addressed slave.
RxACK=1 when no acknowledge is received.

feature_list.requirement_text: BUSY must indicate whether the I2C bus is busy, setting after START detection and clearing after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify BUSY status behavior around START and STOP detection.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe BUSY via SR[6].
test_steps: Ensure the bus is idle and read SR.
Generate or observe a START condition.
Read SR and inspect BUSY.
Generate or observe a STOP condition.
Read SR again and inspect BUSY.
expected_results: BUSY=0 when the bus is idle.
BUSY becomes 1 after START detection.
BUSY returns to 0 after STOP detection.

feature_list.requirement_text: AL must indicate arbitration lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL status bit asserts when arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires creating a valid arbitration-lost condition.
test_steps: Create an arbitration-lost scenario on the I2C bus.
Read SR and inspect AL bit.
expected_results: AL is set when the core loses arbitration.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when a STOP signal is detected but was not requested.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL is set when an unrequested STOP is detected.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires external bus stimulus causing STOP without STO request.
test_steps: Begin a bus activity without issuing STO.
Inject or cause a STOP condition on the bus from another source.
Read SR and inspect AL.
expected_results: AL is set when a STOP is detected that was not requested by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when the master drives SDA high but SDA is low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL is set when SDA observed low conflicts with intended high drive/release.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires multi-master or forced bus contention on SDA.
test_steps: Place the core in a condition where it intends SDA high.
Force or have another master hold SDA low on the bus.
Read SR and inspect AL.
expected_results: AL is set when the core drives/releases SDA high but the observed SDA line is low.

feature_list.requirement_text: TIP must indicate whether a transfer is in progress.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TIP status behavior during and after transfers.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe TIP via SR[1].
test_steps: Initiate an I2C transfer.
Read SR during active transfer and inspect TIP.
Read SR after transfer completion and inspect TIP again.
expected_results: TIP=1 while transferring data.
TIP=0 when the transfer is complete.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must indicate whether an interrupt is pending.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF status bit indicates pending interrupt state.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a condition that sets IF and a method to clear it.
test_steps: Create a condition that generates a pending interrupt.
Read SR and inspect IF.
Clear the interrupt using IACK.
Read SR again and inspect IF.
expected_results: IF=1 when an interrupt is pending.
IF clears after interrupt acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: A pending interrupt must cause a processor interrupt request when IEN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify wb_inta_o assertion requires IF pending and IEN enabled.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires generation of IF in both IEN-disabled and IEN-enabled cases.
test_steps: Clear IEN and create an interrupt-pending condition.
Observe wb_inta_o.
Set IEN and create an interrupt-pending condition again.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: With IEN=0, a pending interrupt does not assert processor interrupt request.
With IEN=1, a pending interrupt asserts wb_inta_o.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must be set when one byte transfer has completed or arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF set conditions for byte completion and arbitration loss.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires two separate scenarios: successful byte completion and AL event.
test_steps: Complete a one-byte transfer and read SR.
Inspect IF after byte completion.
Create an arbitration-lost condition and read SR again.
Inspect IF after arbitration loss.
expected_results: IF is set after one byte transfer completes.
IF is set when arbitration is lost.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Status Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR reset value.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check immediately after reset before bus activity.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read SR from address 0x04.
expected_results: SR resets to 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reserved bits read as zero and assess write handling for reserved writable locations.
feature_list.test_constraints: Write-as-zero guidance is partly a software usage rule; hardware reaction to nonzero writes is not fully specified.
test_steps: Read SR and inspect reserved bits SR[4:2].
Write nonzero values into reserved fields of CR[2:1] and CTR[5:0] while preserving defined bits as needed.
Read observable registers back where applicable.
Assess whether reserved bits read as zero.
expected_results: Reserved status bits read as zero.
Readable reserved bits in control paths read as zero where applicable.
Software procedure should write reserved writable bits as zero for forward compatibility.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data must be transferred between a Master and Slave synchronously to SCL on SDA, byte by byte.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data transfer occurs on SDA synchronized to SCL in byte units.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe external I2C bus waveforms.
test_steps: Initiate one or more I2C transfers.
Observe SDA transitions and SCL clocking on the bus.
Group transferred bits by clock cycles into byte units.
expected_results: Data transfer occurs on SDA synchronized to SCL.
Transfer proceeds byte by byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each I2C data byte must be 8 bits long and transmitted most significant bit first.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each data byte is 8 bits and MSB-first.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use known transmitted byte patterns for clear bit-order observation.
test_steps: Load TXR with a known asymmetric pattern.
Initiate a write transfer.
Observe eight data bit periods on SDA relative to SCL.
Compare transmitted order to the programmed byte.
expected_results: Exactly 8 data bits are transferred per byte.
The most significant bit is transmitted first.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each transferred byte must be followed by an acknowledge bit on the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify acknowledge phase occurs on the ninth SCL clock after each byte.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe complete byte plus acknowledge timing on the bus.
test_steps: Initiate a byte transfer.
Count SCL pulses associated with the transfer.
Observe the acknowledge bit phase immediately following the 8 data bits.
expected_results: An acknowledge bit follows each transferred byte.
The acknowledge phase occurs on the ninth SCL clock cycle.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data bits must be sampled during SCL high, and SDA must remain stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA stability during SCL high and data change only during SCL low.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus waveforms during data transfer, excluding START/STOP conditions.
test_steps: Initiate a data transfer.
Observe SDA and SCL waveforms over several bits.
Check that each bit is valid during SCL high.
Check that SDA transitions occur only while SCL is low during data transfer.
expected_results: Data is sampled during SCL high.
SDA remains stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
SDA changes only during SCL low for data bits.

feature_list.requirement_text: A standard I2C communication must follow the sequence START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the standard I2C communication sequence on the bus.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a complete standard transaction.
test_steps: Initiate a standard transaction from idle.
Observe the first protocol event on the bus.
Observe the address transfer phase.
Observe one or more data transfer bytes.
Observe the transaction termination.
expected_results: The observed transaction sequence is START, slave address transfer, data transfer, then STOP.

feature_list.requirement_text: A START signal must be a high-to-low transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START condition waveform definition.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus waveform at START event.
test_steps: Trigger a START condition.
Capture SDA and SCL around the event.
Check the SDA transition direction while SCL is high.
expected_results: START is observed as SDA transitioning high-to-low while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: A Repeated START must be a START signal generated without first generating a STOP signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify repeated START occurs without a preceding STOP.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires active transaction context.
test_steps: Begin a transaction and do not generate STOP.
Issue a repeated START command sequence.
Observe bus events between the initial transaction and next START.
expected_results: A START condition is generated without an intervening STOP condition.
This event constitutes a Repeated START.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must generate a START signal when STA is set and either RD or WR is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START generation requires STA together with RD or WR.
feature_list.test_constraints: Test STA+WR and STA+RD cases.
test_steps: Write CR with STA and WR set.
Observe the bus for START generation.
Write CR with STA and RD set in an appropriate scenario.
Observe the bus for START generation.
expected_results: The core generates START when STA and WR are set.
The core generates START when STA and RD are set.

feature_list.requirement_text: A STOP signal must be a low-to-high transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STOP condition waveform definition.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus waveform at STOP event.
test_steps: Trigger a STOP condition.
Capture SDA and SCL around the event.
Check the SDA transition direction while SCL is high.
expected_results: STOP is observed as SDA transitioning low-to-high while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: The first byte transferred by the master after a START signal must be the slave address with a seven-bit address followed by an RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify first post-START byte is 7-bit slave address plus RW bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use known slave address and observe first byte after START.
test_steps: Initiate a transaction with a known 7-bit slave address and RW value.
Observe the byte immediately following START.
Decode the first seven bits and the final bit.
expected_results: The first byte after START is the slave address transfer.
Bits [7:1] represent the 7-bit slave address and bit [0] represents RW.

feature_list.requirement_text: The addressed slave must acknowledge by pulling SDA low at the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify addressed slave acknowledge timing at the ninth clock.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires an addressed slave that acknowledges.
test_steps: Perform a slave address transfer to a responding slave.
Count SCL cycles for the address byte.
Observe SDA during the ninth clock cycle.
expected_results: The addressed slave pulls SDA low during the ninth SCL clock cycle to acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must support 10-bit slave addresses by generating two address transfers.
feature_list.test_description: Verify 10-bit addressing support through two address transfers.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires 10-bit address test scenario and bus observation.
test_steps: Prepare a 10-bit slave addressing transaction.
Initiate the addressing sequence.
Observe the bus and count address transfer bytes before data phase.
expected_results: The core performs two address transfers for a 10-bit slave address.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must treat a slave address transfer as a write action using the Transmit Register and WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave address transfer uses TXR data and WR command path.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires bus observation of address transfer after TXR load and WR command.
test_steps: Load the desired slave address byte into TXR.
Issue WR command for the address transfer.
Observe the bus and confirm the address byte is transmitted.
expected_results: Slave address transfer is initiated through TXR plus WR usage.
The programmed address byte is placed on the bus.

feature_list.requirement_text: After successful slave addressing, data transfer must proceed byte by byte in the direction specified by the RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify post-address data phase direction follows RW bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires successful address acknowledgment for both read and write cases.
test_steps: Perform a successfully acknowledged address transfer with RW=0.
Observe that subsequent data transfers proceed in write direction.
Perform a successfully acknowledged address transfer with RW=1.
Observe that subsequent data transfers proceed in read direction.
expected_results: After successful addressing, data proceeds byte by byte.
Transfer direction matches the RW bit value.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave signals No Acknowledge, the master may generate a STOP or Repeated START signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify behavior options after No Acknowledge condition.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement expresses allowed protocol behavior; either STOP or Repeated START path may be exercised.
test_steps: Perform a transfer that results in No Acknowledge from the slave.
After detecting No Acknowledge, issue STO and observe STOP generation.
Repeat No Acknowledge scenario and issue STA with a new command sequence.
Observe Repeated START generation.
expected_results: Following No Acknowledge, the master can generate a STOP.
Following No Acknowledge, the master can generate a Repeated START.

feature_list.requirement_text: To write data to a slave, software must store the data in the Transmit Register and set the WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify software write procedure using TXR and WR produces a data write.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a software usage rule expressed through observable operation.
test_steps: Store a known byte into TXR.
Set the WR bit in CR.
Observe the bus for transmission of the programmed data byte.
expected_results: Using TXR followed by WR causes the specified data byte to be written to the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: To read data from a slave, software must set the RD bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify software read procedure using RD initiates a data read.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a software usage rule expressed through observable operation.
test_steps: Prepare the bus for a slave read transfer.
Set the RD bit in CR.
Observe that the core initiates a read and subsequently captures received data into RXR.
expected_results: Setting RD initiates a read from the slave.
Received data is captured by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: When a transfer is done, the core must reset TIP and set IF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify end-of-transfer status updates for TIP and IF.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe SR after transfer completion.
test_steps: Initiate a transfer and monitor TIP during activity.
Wait for transfer completion.
Read SR and inspect TIP and IF.
expected_results: At transfer completion, TIP is reset to 0.
At transfer completion, IF is set to 1.

feature_list.requirement_text: When interrupt generation is enabled and a transfer is done, the core must generate an interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer completion causes wb_inta_o when IEN is enabled.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires transfer completion under IEN=1.
test_steps: Set IEN in CTR.
Initiate and complete a transfer.
Observe wb_inta_o at transfer completion.
expected_results: When transfer completes with interrupts enabled, wb_inta_o is asserted.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain valid data after IF has been set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR validity once IF is asserted.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires read transfer producing received data.
test_steps: Perform a read transfer from a slave.
Wait until IF is set.
Read RXR from address 0x03.
Compare RXR contents to the received bus byte.
expected_results: After IF is set, RXR contains valid received data.
RXR matches the byte received from the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software may issue a new read or write command when TIP is reset.
feature_list.test_description: Verify new RD/WR command can be issued after TIP deasserts.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a software usage rule validated through observable command acceptance.
test_steps: Complete a transfer and confirm TIP is reset.
Issue a new WR or RD command.
Observe that the new command is accepted and begins execution.
expected_results: When TIP=0, a new read or write command can be issued successfully.

feature_list.requirement_text: When two or more masters try to control the bus simultaneously, clock synchronization must determine the bus clock.
feature_list.test_description: Verify multi-master clock synchronization behavior on SCL.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires multi-master test environment.
test_steps: Set up two masters attempting simultaneous bus control with differing clock timing.
Observe resulting SCL waveform on the shared bus.
Compare bus clock behavior to individual master timing intentions.
expected_results: A clock synchronization process determines the resulting shared bus clock when multiple masters contend.

feature_list.requirement_text: During clock synchronization, SCL must be held low by the device with the longest low period and held high by the device with the shortest high period.
feature_list.test_description: Verify detailed SCL clock synchronization low/high period resolution.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires multi-master test environment with intentionally different low/high timing.
test_steps: Configure two masters with different intended SCL low and high periods.
Start simultaneous bus activity.
Measure the shared SCL low duration and high duration.
Compare observed durations against the two masters' programmed timings.
expected_results: Shared SCL low period matches the longest low period among masters.
Shared SCL high period matches the shortest high period among masters.

feature_list.requirement_text: Slave devices may slow the transfer bit rate by holding SCL low after the master has driven SCL low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify tolerance of slave clock stretching by holding SCL low.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires slave or bus model capable of clock stretching.
test_steps: Initiate an I2C transfer from the master core.
Have the slave hold SCL low after the master drives it low.
Observe the shared SCL waveform and transfer progression.
expected_results: The transfer bit rate is slowed when the slave holds SCL low.
The core tolerates the stretched low period and continues correctly afterward.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave SCL low period is greater than the master SCL low period, the resulting SCL bus low period must be stretched and wait states inserted.
feature_list.test_description: Verify bus low-period stretching and resulting wait-state insertion due to slave clock stretching.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires slave or bus model capable of extending SCL low beyond master low period.
test_steps: Initiate a transfer with known master SCL timing.
Force the slave to hold SCL low longer than the master's intended low period.
Measure the resulting shared SCL low interval.
Observe transfer progression for inserted waiting behavior.
expected_results: The shared SCL low period is stretched beyond the master's intended low period.
Wait-state behavior is effectively inserted until SCL is released by the slave.
